filter_reason: Agent evaluation harnesses are squarely in scope, and this targets a real gap: evaluating proactive agents on real-world tasks rather than sandboxed single-turn setups.
summary: UniClawBench is a new benchmark for 'proactive' agents - assistants that operate everyday tools and take initiative in real-world environments rather than just answering prompts. The authors argue existing agent benchmarks fall short because they rely on sandboxed environments and single-task setups. As proactive, always-on personal agents move from demos to deployments, standardized ways to evaluate them on messy real-world tasks matter to anyone building or comparing agent systems.
title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
excerpt: The rapid development of large language models and multimodal large language models has accelerated the emergence of proactive agents capable of operating everyday tools and assisting users in real-world environments. However, existing benchmarks struggle to evaluate such agents effectively, as they often rely on sandboxed environments and single-turn evaluation paradigms. Moreover, their scenario-based task taxonomies mix multiple model capabilities within the same task category, making it difficult to identify the root causes of agent failures. To address these limitations, we introduce UniC
